questions.question: Which query class does a KV store handle best?
options: Direct fact lookup keyed by (user, type, entity)
Reachability across customers sharing a billing entity
questions.explanation: KV is O(1) on exact keys; vector is for similarity, graph is for relationships.

questions.question: What are the three stores Mem0 writes in parallel on each add()?
questions.explanation: Mem0 fans every write out to vector, KV, and graph stores.

questions.question: What three dimensions feed Mem0's fusion score?
questions.explanation: Score is a weighted sum of relevance, importance, and recency; weights tune per product.

questions.question: What does Mem0g do when an incoming fact contradicts an existing edge?
options: Marks the existing edge invalid but does not delete it, so temporal queries can still traverse
questions.explanation: Soft invalidation preserves history for temporal (valid-at-time) queries.

questions.question: Why does the lesson recommend tuning fusion weights per product?
options: Recency dominates for chat agents while importance dominates for compliance agents and relevance dominates for retrieval agents
questions.explanation: Different products want different bias on relevance/importance/recency; one set of weights does not fit all.

questions.question: What is the scope taxonomy Mem0 uses?
questions.explanation: Scopes are user (cross-session), session (one thread), agent (per-instance state).

questions.question: What is embedding drift in this pattern, and how does the lesson recommend mitigating it?
options: Vector retrieval quality degrades as the corpus grows; periodically re-embed the top-N most used records
Embeddings overflow integers; switch to float64
The embedding API changes URL; pin a domain
questions.explanation: Periodic re-embedding of hot records keeps retrieval quality steady as the corpus grows.
